name: Linux Host Overview (Conduit default)
description: Default host metrics + system logs collected by Conduit's linux profile. Every metric panel is broken down by host.name so multi-host fleets split out automatically; use the Host preset filter at the top to scope to a single machine. Uses percent-form *.utilization metrics (cpu, memory, filesystem, paging) and the parsed log fields (severity_text, process, normalized_message) emitted by the agent.

panels.content:
# Linux Host Overview

Default platform telemetry from **Conduit's linux profile** ([`internal/profiles/linux/`](https://github.com/conduit-obs/conduit-agent/tree/main/internal/profiles/linux)), with every panel broken down by `host.name` for multi-host fleets.

| Section | Source | What it shows |
|---|---|---|
| Load | `metrics` | `system.cpu.load_average.{1m,5m,15m}` (gauge) |
| CPU | `metrics` | `system.cpu.utilization` (0..1 fraction) by `state`, idle filtered out |
| Memory | `metrics` | `system.memory.utilization` (0..1 fraction) by `state` |
| Filesystem | `metrics` | `system.filesystem.utilization` (0..1 fraction) restricted to block-device-backed mounts (filters out `tmpfs`, `cgroup`, `proc`, etc.) |
| Disk | `metrics` | `system.disk.io` (cumulative sum, auto-INCREASE) by `device` + `direction` |
| Network | `metrics` | `system.network.io` (cumulative sum) excluding `lo`; `system.network.connections` by TCP state |
| Logs | `prod-infra` | Volume by `severity_text` and template counts via the parsed `process` + `normalized_message` columns. Filelog-parsed lines from `/var/log/{syslog,messages,auth.log,secure}` get the structured columns; journald entries (no regex parser) appear in the severity panel only. |
| Paging | `metrics` | `system.paging.utilization` — Linux-only addition over the macOS board, since swap usage is meaningful on most Linux deployments. |

**Filter** by `Host` to scope all panels to one machine. **Filter** by `Service` if you run multiple Conduit services per host (rare).

**Reading the utilization charts**: values are 0..1 fractions. A `system.memory.utilization` of `0.82` means 82% of memory is in the queried `state` (e.g. `used`).

For the cross-platform contract this board satisfies, see [`internal/profiles/PROFILE_SPEC.md`](https://github.com/conduit-obs/conduit-agent/blob/main/internal/profiles/PROFILE_SPEC.md).

_Dashboard generated by Conduit V0 MVP. Stored as a checked-in definition under `dashboards/` in the Conduit repo._

panels.name: Load Average (1m / 5m / 15m) by host
panels.description: Three-window load average from system.cpu.load_average.{1m,5m,15m}. Sustained values above core-count = overloaded host.

panels.name: CPU Utilization (% non-idle) by state, per host
panels.description: AVG of system.cpu.utilization (0..1 fraction) by host.name + state, with state=idle filtered out so the chart shows only user / system / iowait / irq / softirq — i.e. effective CPU load.

panels.name: Memory Utilization by state, per host
panels.description: AVG of system.memory.utilization (0..1 fraction) by host.name + state. On Linux the states include used, free, buffered, cached, slab_reclaimable, and slab_unreclaimable. Treat 'used + buffered + cached' as the non-evictable footprint when reasoning about pressure.

panels.name: Filesystem Utilization (% used) by mount, per host
panels.description: MAX of system.filesystem.utilization (0..1 fraction) restricted to block-device-backed mounts (device starts-with /dev/) and state=used, so tmpfs / cgroup / proc / sys / overlay don't clutter the chart. Operators with custom mounts can clone this panel and widen the filter.

panels.name: Disk I/O by Device + Direction (per host)
panels.description: SUM of system.disk.io broken down by host.name + device + direction. Honeycomb auto-applies INCREASE to the cumulative counter so values are bytes-per-bucket.

panels.name: Network Throughput by Interface (per host)
panels.description: SUM of system.network.io broken down by host.name + device + direction, excluding the loopback interface. Container / overlay interfaces (veth*, docker*, br-*, cni*) come through by default — operators in heavy-container fleets should clone this panel and add their interface-prefix filter.

panels.name: TCP Connections by State (per host)
panels.description: AVG of system.network.connections by state. ESTABLISHED + LISTEN dominate on a healthy box; growing CLOSE_WAIT is usually a leak in user code; sustained TIME_WAIT spikes after deploys are usually a connection-pool config issue.

panels.name: Log Volume by Severity (per host)
panels.description: COUNT of prod-infra events broken down by host.name + severity_text. Filelog-parsed lines from /var/log/{syslog,messages,auth.log,secure} arrive with severity_text=INFO by default (set by Conduit's transform/logs processor); journald entries keep whatever severity they carried (PRIORITY -> severity).

panels.name: Top Log Templates (per host)
panels.description: COUNT of prod-infra events grouped by host.name + process + normalized_message, top 25. Uses the masked normalized_message column (UUIDs, IPs, key=value values, and 4+ digit numbers replaced with *), so each row is a distinct log template rather than a unique line. journald entries are excluded here because the regex parser only runs on filelog-parsed lines — see the severity panel for journald volume.

panels.name: Swap (Paging) Utilization by state, per host
panels.description: AVG of system.paging.utilization (0..1 fraction) by host.name + state. Linux-only addition over the macOS board: sustained swap-out usage is usually a sign of memory pressure that the memory utilization panel alone won't catch (especially when the kernel is paging anonymous memory before evicting page cache).
